author: mark
text:
claim: optional remote-sync for global ordering

Add `require_remote_on_claim` config, gating a push of the freshly
committed claim through `origin/balls/tasks` before the worktree is
created. Push rejects fetch-and-merge against the remote and use
the existing field-level merge in `resolve.rs` to pick a winner —
earliest `updated_at` wins per the existing rule. Lost claims fail
loudly with no worktree; unreachable remote is a hard fail rather
than a silent local-only fallback.

Layering: repo-default `.balls/config.json`, per-clone
`.balls/local/config.json`, per-invocation `--sync` / `--no-sync`.
`bl prime` shows a one-time hint when a fresh clone first sees a
remote-set policy. Off by default, so solo and offline workflows
are unchanged.

Tests cover the loop's state transitions (closure-driven
`run_push_loop`) and the wired claim path (race lost, unreachable
remote, repo-default policy, local override, CLI overrides,
no-worktree path).
